_meta.scenario: Q1 2025 Company OKRs — used as the active baseline for drift detection
_meta.notes: These OKRs are deliberately focused on activation and retention (making existing users more successful). Use these to show how the drift agent catches work that drifts toward acquisition — a different strategy entirely.

content.context: Following a Q4 2024 board review, Nexus is shifting focus from top-of-funnel growth to monetization efficiency. CAC is up 40% YoY while LTV has been flat. Q1 2025 is a deliberate 'consolidation quarter': ship nothing new for new users — instead, make existing users successful enough to expand and renew.

objectives.title: Dramatically improve new user activation
objectives.description: Too many trial users sign up and never reach their first 'aha moment.' We lose 72% of trials before they invite a second team member. Fix the funnel, not the top.

keyResults.description: Increase trial-to-paid conversion rate from 18% to 28%
keyResults.measurementSource: Stripe + CRM: paid conversion event within 14 days of trial start

keyResults.description: Reduce median time-to-first-value from 4.2 days to 2 days (first meaningful action: invite teammate OR publish first report)
keyResults.measurementSource: Mixpanel: median time from `signup_completed` to `first_value_action` event

keyResults.description: Achieve 60% completion rate on the onboarding checklist (currently 31%)

objectives.title: Improve 30-day and 90-day retention for paid accounts
objectives.description: Our 30-day retention is 42% — well below the SaaS B2B benchmark of 60%+. Users who reach week 4 have an 84% chance of renewing. The problem is getting them to week 4.

keyResults.description: Raise 30-day retention for paid users from 42% to 55%
keyResults.measurementSource: Mixpanel: 30-day retention cohort report, paid users only, monthly cohorts

keyResults.description: Reduce monthly churn rate from 6.8% to 4.5%
Increase the proportion of paid users who are 'highly engaged' (≥3 logins/week + ≥1 collaboration action) from 28% to 45%

objectives.title: Make the platform reliable enough that reliability is never a churn reason
objectives.description: In Q4 2024, 3 enterprise accounts cited reliability concerns in exit interviews. Zero tolerance for reliability-driven churn in Q1.

keyResults.description: Achieve 99.9% uptime (max 43 minutes downtime in Q1)
Reduce P95 API latency from 340ms to under 200ms
keyResults.measurementSource: Datadog APM: p95 latency, 7-day trailing, all production endpoints

explicitlyNotInStrategy: New acquisition channels or paid marketing campaigns — CAC efficiency is a Q2 initiative
New enterprise sales motions — Sales team is in hiring freeze through Q1
New integrations for prospect-facing demos — no new integration work unless it directly improves retention
Public-facing product launches or press announcements — no launch distractions in Q1
